parity: PASS in-repo (102/102 selected, terminal-block 100%, transplant-support 100%, deterministic, no mutation)
CRITICAL_FINDING: V3 transplant signal is INERT live: 0 overrides / 0 blocks across all S3 matchups (vs c3 8-24 overrides), 0 errors.
root_cause: runtime _v3_resolve_transplant_support builds table_key = f"{family}||{_semantic_action_key(option)}" where _semantic_action_key returns the STRUCTURED JSON key; but the exported transplant_support_table.json is keyed with the COMPACT form (e.g. "ATTACH||ATTACH:Water:Mega"). The structured key never matches a compact table key -> every lookup returns missing -> fallback -> baseline.
why_parity_passed: Model A parity inputs SUPPLY transplant support directly (observation.transplant_support_by_semantic_key + per-option transplant on all 21 selector-source rows), bypassing the table. Parity never exercises the table fallback, so the key-format bug was not caught.
why_model_b_cannot_work_around: Model B has no transplant-scoring function (that is Model A V0); computing/attaching support would be the forbidden "substitute a different transplant support calculation". Re-keying Model A export silently is also out of scope. So the live path has no working support provider.
safety: V3 is SAFE: fail-closed to baseline, never overrides into ATTACK/END/RETREAT, 0 errors/illegal. Parity-clean.
smoke_not_run_full: A full 360-game smoke was NOT run because S3 is provably inert (== off) live; it would only reproduce baseline and cannot test the transplant policy. A tiny confirmation (c3 vs v3) established the inertness.
verdict_scope: INVALID FOR LIVE TRANSPLANT TEST (not unsafe). The export is parity-clean and fail-closed safe, but the transplant layer does not reach the live runtime, so V3 == off live and the smoke cannot evaluate it.
fix_for_model_a: Make the runtime table-lookup key match the table key format: either (a) derive the COMPACT semantic key for the table lookup, or (b) re-export transplant_support_table.json keyed by the STRUCTURED _semantic_action_key the runtime already computes.
OR make the runtime self-contained: compute transplant support from packed features without requiring caller-supplied support maps.
Add a parity probe that exercises the TABLE fallback path (no supplied support map) so this is caught next time.
